name: Lantz, Olague, San Urbano, Bosque de Orgi, Aranbide
name_original: Lantz, Olague, San Urbano, Bosque de Orgi, Aranbide – Ruta Circular
description: This difficult 20.3-mile touring cycling route through the Ultzama Valley offers diverse terrain and stunning natural beauty.
region_intro: Hiking around Lantz offers trails through mountainous terrain, characterized by summits, passes, and varied landscapes. The region features routes with significant elevation changes, providing diverse experiences for hikers. These trails often lead to prominent peaks and historical sites like hermitages, showcasing the natural and cultural features of the area. The network includes routes ranging from moderate to difficult, catering to different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Lantz The most popular hiking route is Santiago Hermitage – Summit of Sayoa loop from Belateko mendatea, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain to a prominent summit. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puerto de Velate – Pie de Txaruta loop from Belateko mendatea, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.5 km) path. This trail offers a balanced experience through varied terrain, taking approximately 3 hours 35 minutes. Local hikers also love the Okolin Summit – Santiago Hermitage loop from Belateko mendatea, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Lantz is defined by mountain passes, challenging summits, and scenic loops. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate ascents to difficult long-distance trails. The routes in Lantz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Lantz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lantz?
A: There are over 70 hiking trails around Lantz, offering a wide range of experiences from moderate ascents to challenging long-distance routes through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Lantz?
A: Hiking around Lantz is characterized by mountainous terrain, featuring prominent summits, scenic passes, and varied landscapes. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, leading to peaks and historical sites like hermitages.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Lantz?
A: Yes, Lantz offers a selection of easier trails. There are 16 routes classified as easy. For a balanced experience, you might consider one of the many moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31909605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Velate – Pie de Txaruta loop from Belateko mendatea&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.8 miles (12.5 km) with moderate elevation changes.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Lantz?
A: The region around Lantz is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1064398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orgi Forest&lt;/a&gt;, conquer the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Sayoa&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3373978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leateko Gaina Rock Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also lead through picturesque mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/985119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artesiaga Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Belate&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Lantz?
A: Yes, many of the popular trails around Lantz are circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago Hermitage – Summit of Sayoa loop from Belateko mendatea&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20517528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Okolin Summit – Santiago Hermitage loop from Belateko mendatea&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Lantz?
A: The trails in Lantz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the challenging summits, the scenic mountain passes, and the diverse landscapes that offer rewarding experiences for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options around Lantz?
A: While the guide focuses on day hikes, some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Sayoa – Lantz loop from Lantz&lt;/a&gt;, extend over 13 miles (20 km) with significant elevation gain, offering a more strenuous, longer-duration experience for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lantz?
A: Given the mountainous nature of the region, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be excellent, though some higher elevation trails might be warm. Winter hiking is possible but may require specialized gear due to snow and ice in higher passes.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Lantz suitable for families?
A: While many trails involve significant elevation and can be challenging, there are 16 easy routes that might be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous outing. Always check the specific trail details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Lantz?
A: Many popular trailheads in mountainous regions like Lantz typically have designated parking areas, especially near well-known starting points such as Belateko mendatea. It's advisable to check specific route information on komoot for details on parking availability for your chosen trail.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Lantz using public transport?
A: Access to mountainous trailheads by public transport can vary. While some larger towns or passes might have bus services, direct public transport to all remote trailheads is not always guaranteed. For specific routes, it's best to consult local transport schedules or consider carpooling to reach starting points like Belateko mendatea.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Lantz, Olague, San Urbano, Bosque de Orgi, Aranbide cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as difficult for touring cyclists. It requires a very good level of fitness due to a significant elevation gain of 460 meters, which includes substantial climbs throughout the 32.7 km course.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists can expect a varied terrain typical of the Ultzama Valley. It features a network of roads and tracks with low traffic, including sections of authentic gravel routes. Within the Orgi Forest, the trails are generally easy, flat, and accessible, but the overall route includes more challenging ascents and diverse surfaces outside this woodland section.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 32.7 km touring bicycle route is approximately 2 hours and 19 minutes. However, actual completion time can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural beauty and cultural highlights. Key points include the ancient Orgi Forest, an 80-hectare humid oak forest of ecological interest, the San Urbano Hermitage, and Aranbide Park. You'll also experience the lush green landscapes, meadows, and forests of the picturesque Ultzama Valley.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Lantz, Olague, San Urbano, Bosque de Orgi, Aranbide cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact trailhead are not provided, Lantz village or other nearby villages like Olague or Aranbide would be suitable starting points with potential parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring or autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures and pleasant weather conditions, making the challenging climbs more enjoyable. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse conditions.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome in the Ultzama Valley, but specific rules apply, especially within protected areas like the Orgi Forest Natural Recreation Area. It is recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Bosque de Orgi Natural Recreation Area?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through the Orgi Forest Natural Recreation Area, there is typically no entrance fee for general access to the forest trails. However, specific activities or facilities within the park might have associated costs. It's always a good idea to check the official website (e.g., visitnavarra.es) for the latest regulations or permit requirements if you plan any special activities.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. Notably, about 25% of the route follows the Baztan Way. Other smaller sections include Orgiko basoaren ibilaldia, Bidezarra, Ollargaingo bidea, and various other local paths like Lizasobidea and Orgibidea.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near several villages such as Lantz, Olague, San Urbano, and Aranbide. These villages, particularly Lantz and Olague, offer local amenities including cafes, pubs, and guesthouses or rural accommodation options where you can refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring a well-maintained touring bicycle, appropriate cycling gear, a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app). Sun protection and layers of clothing are also recommended due to potential weather changes.
